g.data-narration: Welcome to this hands-on guide about Ethereum Clients and Wallets, focusing on Geth.
An Ethereum client is software that connects to the blockchain network, validating and propagating transactions.
An Ethereum wallet manages user accounts, enabling sending and receiving of ETH and ERC-20 tokens securely.
Geth is the official Go implementation of Ethereum, serving as both client and wallet with CLI interface.
Geth supports Web3.js API, RPC protocol, and WebSocket for flexible blockchain interaction methods.
Geth architecture consists of three main components: Node (protocol), Database (storage), and Web Interface.
Geth uses Block and Transaction data structures to organize and verify blockchain information.
Installing Geth involves downloading from the official Ethereum website and following OS-specific instructions.
Geth uses config.json to specify network ID, gas price, gas limit, and account management settings.
Geth RPC enables remote command execution on Ethereum, supporting transaction sending and balance retrieval.
Common Geth CLI commands include datadir for data storage, RPC flags for network communication.
Additional Geth commands enable WebSocket connections, testnet mode, and synchronization settings.
Ethereum uses SHA-256 and Keccak-256 hash functions for secure transaction and block verification.
Ethereum uses Proof of Work (legacy) and Proof of Stake (Ethereum 2.0) consensus mechanisms.
Best practices include using strong passwords, keeping Geth updated, secure networks, and careful RPC usage.
Common Geth issues include syncing problems, network connectivity, and RPC errors - all with solutions.
g.text: Troubleshooting: Sync Issues | Network Connection | RPC Errors
